content.full_text:
i

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 1, 1992

Hamid E. Gabbay A.I.A
GABBAY ARCHITECTS
448 South Canon Drive, Suite 200
Beverly Hills, CA 90212

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-92-357

Dear Mr. Gabbay:

As a Planning Commissioner for the City of Beverly Hills, you
have requested advice regarding the gift provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

QUESTION

Is an airplane ticket, which your client purchases for you to
visit a job site, considered income or a gift?

CONCLUSION

The airplane ticket, which is provided in connection with
your bona fide business, is reportable income.

FACTS

You are an architect and a partner in the firm of Gabbay
Architects. Your firm has projects in other states and you often
have to visit the job sites. The airplane ticket for such travel
is usually purchased by the client.

ANALYSIS

Section 82030(a) defines income as both a payment received
and reimbursement for expenses. Regulation 18950.2(f) (copy
enclosed) states that a payment made for transportation or
necessary accommodations in connection with a bona fide business,

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-92-357
Page 2


trade or profession is not an honorarium or a gift, unless the
sole or predominant activity of the business, trade, or profession
is making speeches.

Therefore, the airplane ticket purchased by your client is
income received in connection with your architectural business.
Any payments for travel must be reported on your Form 721 as
income.

Please also note that any source of income over $250 in a 12
month period may trigger a conflict of interest if a decision by
the Beverly Hills Planning Commission will have a material
financial effect on the source of income. (See Section 87103.)

I trust this answers your question.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Jill Stecher
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JS:aa

Enclosure
